title: Curvature-locking functional
definition: Curvature-locking functional is the canonical M1 object in the Master systems family.
formula:
\sigma(s):=\sqrt{\kappa(s)^2+\tau(s)^2}, \qquad \langle f\rangle_w:= \frac{\oint_\Gamma w f\,ds}{\oint_\Gamma w\,ds}, \quad \oint_\Gamma w\,ds>0.

S_{\rm lock}[\varphi] = \oint_\Gamma w(s)\bigl(\partial_s\varphi-\sigma\bigr)^2\,ds.

m=\frac{\hbar}{c}\langle\sigma\rangle_w.

title: Nonsingular curvature operator and Lyapunov candidate
definition: Nonsingular curvature operator and Lyapunov candidate is the canonical M2 object in the Master systems family. The denominator is strictly positive for every complex \(\psi\).

title: Finite-band spectral selector
definition: Finite-band spectral selector is the canonical M3 object in the Master systems family. Its Fourier growth symbol contains so sufficiently off-shell ultraviolet modes are damped.

title: Dimensional stability threshold
definition: Dimensional stability threshold is the canonical M4 object in the Master systems family. For a standard Sobolev domain, For integer spatial dimension,

title: Curvature-bounded computation
definition: Curvature-bounded computation is the canonical M5 object in the Master systems family. Complexity claims require a specified encoding, precision model, update cost, and finite curvature-resource bound.

title: Unified linear operator
definition: Unified linear operator is the canonical M6A object in the Master systems family.

title: Nonlinear curvature operator
definition: Nonlinear curvature operator is the canonical M6B object in the Master systems family. The operator is well-defined for \(\varepsilon>0\); uniqueness of this nonlinear closure remains open.

title: Full curvature-wavefield equation
definition: Full curvature-wavefield equation is the canonical M7 object in the Master systems family. The explicit negative fourth-order term supplies finite-band ultraviolet damping. Other dynamical claims require separate analysis.

title: Curvature-acoustic cosmology system
definition: Curvature-acoustic cosmology system is the canonical M8 object in the Master systems family. Representative closure relations are The full cosmology sector remains open pending derivation, parameter closure, and calibrated data tests.

title: Curvature-rate density
family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
definition: Curvature-rate density is the canonical E1A object in the A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking family.

title: Curvature spectral rate
family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
definition: Curvature spectral rate is the canonical E1B object in the A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking family.

title: Weighted loop average
family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
definition: Weighted loop average is the canonical E2 object in the A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking family.

title: Loop-locking action
family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
formulas: S_{\rm eff}[\varphi] = \oint_\Gamma w(\partial_s\varphi-\sigma)^2\,ds + \Lambda \left( \oint_\Gamma\partial_s\varphi\,ds-2\pi n \right), \qquad n\in\mathbb Z.
definition: Loop-locking action is the canonical E3 object in the A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking family. For \(w\ge0\), the mismatch term is nonnegative.
formula: S_{\rm eff}[\varphi] = \oint_\Gamma w(\partial_s\varphi-\sigma)^2\,ds + \Lambda \left( \oint_\Gamma\partial_s\varphi\,ds-2\pi n \right), \qquad n\in\mathbb Z.

title: Covariant locking solution
family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
definition: Covariant locking solution is the canonical E4 object in the A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking family. Stationarity gives where

title: Effective-wavenumber chain
family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
definition: Effective-wavenumber chain is the canonical E5 object in the A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking family.
formula:
L_s:=\oint_\Gamma ds, \qquad k_{\rm wind}:=\frac{2\pi|n|}{L_s}, \qquad k_\sigma:=\frac1{L_s}\oint_\Gamma\sigma\,ds.

k_{\rm wind}=k_\sigma=\langle\sigma\rangle_w

title: Mass-curvature law
family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
formulas: E_{\rm rest}=\hbar c\,k_{\rm eff}, \qquad m=\frac{\hbar}{c}k_{\rm eff}.
definition: Mass-curvature law is the canonical E6 object in the A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking family.
formula:
E_{\rm rest}=\hbar c\,k_{\rm eff}, \qquad m=\frac{\hbar}{c}k_{\rm eff}.

m=\frac{\hbar}{c}\langle\sigma\rangle_w.

title: Solenoidal mass law
family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
definition: Solenoidal mass law is the canonical E7 object in the A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking family. The averaging measure must be specified; the dimensional relation is valid.

title: Corrected weighted-lock identity
family: A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking
definition: Corrected weighted-lock identity is the canonical E8 object in the A. Rest energy, curvature, and loop locking family. Substituting E4 gives The earlier extra factor

title: Phase-flux constitutive relation
definition: Phase-flux constitutive relation is the canonical E9 object in the B. Phase-flux and finite-band selection family. A conservation equation, when imposed, is The constitutive relation itself is a definition.

title: Radial shell quantization
definition: Radial shell quantization is the canonical E10 object in the B. Phase-flux and finite-band selection family. Both sides are dimensionless.

title: Phase winding
definition: Phase winding is the canonical E11 object in the B. Phase-flux and finite-band selection family. provided \(\psi\neq0\) on the loop and the phase is continuous modulo \(2\pi\).

title: Finite-band dispersion rail
definition: Finite-band dispersion rail is the canonical E12 object in the B. Phase-flux and finite-band selection family. To avoid conflict with curvature \(\sigma\), write the growth rate as Its nonzero stationary maximum is

title: Band-pass amplitude evolution
definition: Band-pass amplitude evolution is the canonical E13 object in the B. Phase-flux and finite-band selection family. A form consistent with E12 is or equivalently

title: Band-pass Lyapunov functional
definition: Band-pass Lyapunov functional is the canonical E14 object in the B. Phase-flux and finite-band selection family. For the centered Swift-Hohenberg form, Gradient-flow descent requires compatible boundary conditions and normalization conventions.

title: Modal growth bound
definition: Modal growth bound is the canonical E15 object in the B. Phase-flux and finite-band selection family. The quartic modal estimate requires a model-specific nonlinear projection bound.

title: Linear spectral concentration
definition: Linear spectral concentration is the canonical E16 object in the B. Phase-flux and finite-band selection family. For the linearized dynamics, If the maximizing shell is isolated and initially populated,

title: Nonsingular curvature-feedback operator
family: C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics
definition: Nonsingular curvature-feedback operator is the canonical E17 object in the C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics family. For \(\varepsilon>0\), the denominator is strictly positive for all complex \(\psi\). This replaces whose denominator can vanish.

title: WCT Lyapunov candidate
family: C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics
definition: WCT Lyapunov candidate is the canonical E18 object in the C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics family. It is nonnegative when \(c1,c2\ge0\). Lyapunov descent still requires a specified evolution equation satisfying \(d\mathcal E/dt\le0\).

title: Gap-curvature scaling
family: C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics
definition: Gap-curvature scaling is the canonical E19 object in the C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics family. Separate the spatial and frequency gaps: The proportionality constant and spectral derivation remain model-dependent.

title: Higher-order cavity quadratic sector
family: C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics
definition: Higher-order cavity quadratic sector is the canonical E20 object in the C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics family. The quadratic sector is
formula:
S:=\frac{\Box\psi}{g(\psi)}, \qquad P:=\frac{\Delta\psi}{g(\psi)}.

Q(S,P)=\kappa S^2+\theta P^2-\gamma SP.

\kappa\ge0,\qquad \theta\ge0,\qquad \gamma^2\le4\kappa\theta.

title: Second-derivative Euler-Lagrange equation
family: C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics
definition: Second-derivative Euler-Lagrange equation is the canonical E21 object in the C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics family.

title: Effective metric ansatz
family: C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics
definition: Effective metric ansatz is the canonical E22 object in the C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics family. The coefficients must carry the units needed to make both corrections dimensionless, and signature/nondegeneracy must be checked.

title: Enthalpic curvature relation
family: C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics
definition: Enthalpic curvature relation is the canonical E23 object in the C. Curvature feedback and Lyapunov dynamics family. The constants must reconcile dimensions and the relation requires a constitutive derivation.

title: Sobolev embedding threshold
definition: Sobolev embedding threshold is the canonical E24 object in the D. Dimensionality and functional bounds family.

title: Critical Sobolev exponent
definition: Critical Sobolev exponent is the canonical E25 object in the D. Dimensionality and functional bounds family. For \(n>2\), is the stated subcriticality condition.

title: Corrected curvature $L^2$ bound
definition: Corrected curvature $L^2$ bound is the canonical E26 object in the D. Dimensionality and functional bounds family.

title: Finite-energy confinement
definition: Finite-energy confinement is the canonical E27 object in the D. Dimensionality and functional bounds family.

family: E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning
definition: Corrected alpha-drop exponent is the canonical E28 object in the E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning family. \(\rhot(n)\in(0,1]\) be retained fractions. Define Then \(\alpha(n)<1\) requires

family: E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning
definition: Entropy-drop pruning is the canonical E29 object in the E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning family.

family: E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning
definition: Conditional entropy-production bound is the canonical E31 object in the E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning family. the entropy drop A noncircular model-specific bound has the form

family: E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning
definition: Subexponential exploration condition is the canonical E32 object in the E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning family. This follows only if the retained-fraction and \(\beta(n)\) bounds in E28 hold uniformly with sufficient margin.

title: Corrected support-entropy relation
family: E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning
definition: Corrected support-entropy relation is the canonical E33 object in the E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning family. For a distribution supported on \(Kt\) modes, hence

family: E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning
definition: Energy-entropy conversion is the canonical E34 object in the E. Alpha-drop, entropy reduction, and pruning family. For entropy reduction the proposed cost relation is
formula:
\Delta H_k:=H_{\rm before}-H_{\rm after}\ge0,

\Delta E_{\rm cost}\ge\lambda\,\Delta H_k, \qquad \lambda>0.

definition: Discrete WCC update is the canonical E36 object in the F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity family.

family: F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity
definition: Bandlimit from energy is the canonical E37 object in the F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity family. with dimensionless \(C1\).

title: Spatial channel capacity
family: F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity
definition: Spatial channel capacity is the canonical E38 object in the F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity family. In three spatial dimensions, with dimensionless \(C2\).

family: F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity
definition: Polynomial update bound is the canonical E39 object in the F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity family. This defines the assumed computational resource class.

formulas: P_{\rm WCC}\cong P, \qquad NP_{\rm WCC}\cong NP.
definition: WCC complexity identification is the canonical E40 object in the F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity family. The identification requires an explicit encoding and polynomial simulation in both directions.
formula: P_{\rm WCC}\cong P, \qquad NP_{\rm WCC}\cong NP.

title: Curvature-bounded configuration count
family: F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity
definition: Curvature-bounded configuration count is the canonical E41 object in the F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity family. E28 alone does not prove this counting bound; an injective coding or combinatorial argument is required.

family: F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity
definition: Theta-information relation is the canonical E42 object in the F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity family. The information functional and coupling \(\lambdaI\) require derivation.

family: F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity
definition: Curvature-entropy tradeoff is the canonical E43 object in the F. WCC, channel capacity, and complexity family. This remains an analytic/empirical claim.

family: G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure
definition: Theta eigenmode problem is the canonical E44 object in the G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure family. Because \(\Theta\varepsilon\) is nonlinear, the spectral problem and normalization must be specified carefully.

family: G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure
definition: Corrected quality factor is the canonical E45 object in the G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure family. where is stored energy and \(P{\rm loss}\) is loss power. An integral of energy density over a loss region is not a power unless a loss rate is included.

family: G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure
definition: Plasma-cavity curvature match is the canonical E46 object in the G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure family. A measurable matching tolerance and transfer mechanism remain open.

family: G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure
formulas: \boxed{ \frac{dW}{dt} = P_{\rm in} + P_{\rm fusion} - P_{\rm loss} - P_{\rm out} }.
P_{\rm in}+P_{\rm fusion} = P_{\rm loss}+P_{\rm out}.
definition: Corrected power balance is the canonical E47 object in the G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure family. At stationarity, Fusion is a source, not a loss term.
formula:
\boxed{ \frac{dW}{dt} = P_{\rm in} + P_{\rm fusion} - P_{\rm loss} - P_{\rm out} }.

P_{\rm in}+P_{\rm fusion} = P_{\rm loss}+P_{\rm out}.

definition: Curvature-gap stability criterion is the canonical E48 object in the G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure family. The threshold and direction of the inequality must be calibrated to a specified stability observable.

definition: Resonance-lock condition is the canonical E54 object in the G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure family. Simultaneous satisfaction requires existence and regularity results.

definition: Curvature-induced effective potential is the canonical E55 object in the G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure family.

definition: Phase-wall criterion is the canonical E56 object in the G. Cavity, effective mass, and phase structure family. The comparison scale and wall-detection threshold must be defined.

definition: Swift-Hohenberg shell operator is the canonical E57 object in the H. Swift-Hohenberg and spectral projection family. Its Fourier symbol is

definition: Band-selective Green kernel is the canonical E58 object in the H. Swift-Hohenberg and spectral projection family. For the Fourier Green kernel is

\mathcal A^\star := \left\{ k\in\mathbb Z^d: \bigl||k|-k_\star\bigr|\le\Delta k \right\},

(P_{k_\star}A)(x) = \sum_{k\in\mathcal A^\star} \widehat A_ke^{ik\cdot x}.

P_{k_\star}^2=P_{k_\star}.

definition: Center-manifold amplitude equation is the canonical E60 object in the H. Swift-Hohenberg and spectral projection family.

definition: Pattern-formation threshold is the canonical E61 object in the H. Swift-Hohenberg and spectral projection family. In the continuum, For a discrete domain, equality requires an admissible mode on the selected shell.

definition: Critical Sobolev exponent is the canonical E65 object in the I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds family.

family: I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds
definition: Gagliardo-Nirenberg interpolation is the canonical E66 object in the I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds family. The allowed \(p,\theta,n\), domain, and boundary assumptions must be specified.

family: I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds
definition: Failure of $H^2 o L^\infty$ above three dimensions is the canonical E67 object in the I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds family. For \(n>3\), the embedding \(H^2\hookrightarrow L^\infty\) fails in general; equivalently, there exist \(H^2\) functions not controlled in \(L^\infty\).

title: Localized energy estimate
family: I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds
definition: Localized energy estimate is the canonical E68 object in the I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds family. A model-dependent localized estimate is Its exponent and constant require a precise scaling regime and domain hypotheses.

title: Corrected high-regularity curvature bound
family: I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds
definition: Corrected high-regularity curvature bound is the canonical E69 object in the I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds family. If and the regularized reciprocal is uniformly bounded, then Sobolev embedding gives

title: Dimensional stability criterion
family: I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds
definition: Dimensional stability criterion is the canonical E70 object in the I. Sobolev structure and dimensional bounds family. Use the one-way criterion as separate hypotheses for the proposed stability analysis. They are not a proven biconditional characterization of all stable WCT solutions.

title: Physical computation resource bound
definition: Physical computation resource bound is the canonical E71 object in the J. Computational resource bounds family. The constant must have units of time, and the interpretation depends on the update rate, precision, and physical encoding.

title: Curvature-pruned search space
definition: Curvature-pruned search space is the canonical E72 object in the J. Computational resource bounds family. A counting theorem linking the physical pruning process to discrete configurations is required.

title: Polynomial verification
definition: Polynomial verification is the canonical E73 object in the J. Computational resource bounds family.

title: Curvature separation conjecture
definition: Curvature separation conjecture is the canonical E74 object in the J. Computational resource bounds family. The finite-size families \(Pn,NPn\) must first be defined.

title: Physical-oracle impossibility
definition: Physical-oracle impossibility is the canonical E75 object in the J. Computational resource bounds family. This is a complexity claim requiring a formal computational model and reduction.

title: WCC complexity equivalence
formulas: P_{\rm WCC}=P \quad\Longrightarrow\quad \text{WCC polynomially simulates the declared physical-computation model}.
definition: WCC complexity equivalence is the canonical E76 object in the J. Computational resource bounds family. The implication requires explicit translations and cost bounds.
formula: P_{\rm WCC}=P \quad\Longrightarrow\quad \text{WCC polynomially simulates the declared physical-computation model}.

title: Mutual-information decay
definition: Mutual-information decay is the canonical E77 object in the K. Entropy and information dynamics family. The probability law, channel, and regularity assumptions remain open.

title: Fisher-information curvature bound
definition: Fisher-information curvature bound is the canonical E78 object in the K. Entropy and information dynamics family. A common probability density and geometric derivation are required.

title: Entropy-production rate
definition: Entropy-production rate is the canonical E79 object in the K. Entropy and information dynamics family. The sign convention and units must be fixed when used physically.

title: Landauer-type bound
definition: Landauer-type bound is the canonical E80 object in the K. Entropy and information dynamics family. If \(\Delta H{\rm bits}\) is measured in bits, For entropy in nats, omit the factor \(\ln2\).

title: Corrected coherence length
definition: Corrected coherence length is the canonical E81 object in the K. Entropy and information dynamics family. With normalized spectral weights \(pk\), Equivalently, when the integrals exist,

title: Information-geometry tensor
definition: Information-geometry tensor is the canonical E82 object in the K. Entropy and information dynamics family. Positive definiteness and coordinate invariance require additional conditions.

title: Curvature-locking functional
definition: Curvature-locking functional is the canonical CLE1 object in the Curvature-locking equations family. Use the inverse-length convention \([\sigma\star]=L^{-1}\):

title: Corrected curvature-lock Euler-Lagrange equation
definition: Corrected curvature-lock Euler-Lagrange equation is the canonical CLE2 object in the Curvature-locking equations family. For the real one-dimensional reduction the generalized Euler-Lagrange equation is

title: Curvature-locking condition
definition: Curvature-locking condition is the canonical CLE3 object in the Curvature-locking equations family. Both sides have units \(L^{-2}\).

title: Locked-field equation
definition: Locked-field equation is the canonical CLE4 object in the Curvature-locking equations family.

title: Thin/product-torus Laplacian
definition: Thin/product-torus Laplacian is the canonical CLE5 object in the Curvature-locking equations family. Under a flat product or thin-torus approximation, The exact embedded-torus Laplace-Beltrami operator contains metric-dependent terms.

title: Separation ansatz
definition: Separation ansatz is the canonical CLE6 object in the Curvature-locking equations family. For CLE4 and CLE5 give

title: Periodic angular mode family
definition: Periodic angular mode family is the canonical CLE7 object in the Curvature-locking equations family. The periodic reduced equation has the full family

title: Selected torus eigenmode
definition: Selected torus eigenmode is the canonical CLE8 object in the Curvature-locking equations family. is one admissible winding-one mode. Uniqueness requires additional lowest-mode, chirality, normalization, phase, and boundary-selection principles.

title: Electron radius from curvature
definition: Electron radius from curvature is the canonical CLE9 object in the Curvature-locking equations family.

title: Curvature scalar chain
definition: Curvature scalar chain is the canonical CLE10 object in the Curvature-locking equations family.

title: Log-periodic ghost modulation
definition: Log-periodic ghost modulation is the canonical G1 object in the Logarithmic and ghost equations family.

title: Logarithmic field representation
definition: Logarithmic field representation is the canonical EX object in the Logarithmic and ghost equations family. For a positive real field \(\psi>0\), let Then
formula:
u=\ln\psi, \qquad \psi=e^u.

\nabla\psi=e^u\nabla u,

\Delta\psi=e^u(\Delta u+|\nabla u|^2),

\frac{\Delta\psi}{\psi} = \Delta u+|\nabla u|^2.

title: Log-curvature evolution
definition: Log-curvature evolution is the canonical EY object in the Logarithmic and ghost equations family. If then the logarithmic dynamics are equivalent to the diffusion equation in EZ, provided \(\psi=e^u>0\).

title: Cole-Hopf reduction
definition: Cole-Hopf reduction is the canonical EZ object in the Logarithmic and ghost equations family.

title: Filament-localization condition
definition: Filament-localization condition is the canonical FA object in the Logarithmic and ghost equations family. A norm, tolerance, scale, and dynamical derivation are required.

title: Fundamental field evolution
definition: Fundamental field evolution is the canonical CM1 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family. Coefficient dimensions and derivation remain open.

title: Curvature-spectral tilt
definition: Curvature-spectral tilt is the canonical CM2 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family.
formula:
P_{\rm prim}(k)\propto k^{-\alpha_{\rm WCT}},

n_s-1=-\alpha_{\rm WCT},

\alpha_{\rm WCT} = -\frac{d\ln|\Theta(k)|}{d\ln k}.

title: Potential from curvature
definition: Potential from curvature is the canonical CM3 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family.

title: Horizon-entry potential decay
definition: Horizon-entry potential decay is the canonical CM4 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family.

title: Curvature-acoustic oscillators
definition: Curvature-acoustic oscillators is the canonical CM5 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family.

title: Sound speed from curvature feedback
definition: Sound speed from curvature feedback is the canonical CM6 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family. Positivity requires the bracketed factor to be nonnegative.

title: Curvature diffusion
definition: Curvature diffusion is the canonical CM7 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family. A phenomenological damping replacement is

title: Initial conditions
definition: Initial conditions is the canonical CM8 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family. Use CM3 consistently: The earlier extra factor and opposite sign were removed.

title: First-order mode system
definition: First-order mode system is the canonical CM9 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family.

title: Tight-coupling drag
definition: Tight-coupling drag is the canonical CM10 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family.
formula:
\delta_b \leftarrow (1-\varepsilon_{\rm drag})\delta_b + \varepsilon_{\rm drag}\delta_\gamma,

\varepsilon_{\rm drag} = \frac{E_{\rm exch}}{E_{\rm comp}}, \qquad 0\le\varepsilon_{\rm drag}\le1.

title: Curvature damping envelope
definition: Curvature damping envelope is the canonical CM11 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family. Dimensional consistency requires the time-dependent diffusion coefficient to carry units \(L^2/T\).

title: Dimensionless power spectrum
definition: Dimensionless power spectrum is the canonical CM12 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family.

title: Peak metrics
definition: Peak metrics is the canonical CM13 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family.

title: Peak-response interpretation
definition: Peak-response interpretation is the canonical CM14 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family. Proposed qualitative relations:
formula:
\text{faster }\Theta\text{ decay}\Rightarrow s_{ij}\uparrow,

\text{larger compression}\Rightarrow r_{31}\uparrow,

\text{larger radiative fraction}\Rightarrow r_{21}\downarrow.

title: WCT angular scaling
definition: WCT angular scaling is the canonical CM15 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family.
formula:
k_{\rm phys} = \frac{k}{a_{\rm WCT}(t)},

a_{\rm WCT}(t) = \left[ \frac{E_{\rm curv}(0)} {E_{\rm curv}(t)} \right]^{1/3}.

title: Acoustic horizon
definition: Acoustic horizon is the canonical CM16 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family.

title: Curvature-energy closure
formulas: E_{\rm curv}(t) + E_{\rm grad}(t) = E_{\rm tot},
definition: Curvature-energy closure is the canonical CM17 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family. for a closed sector with no external source or loss.
formula: E_{\rm curv}(t) + E_{\rm grad}(t) = E_{\rm tot},

title: Minimal cosmology closure set
definition: Minimal cosmology closure set is the canonical CM18 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family. This is a bookkeeping closure, not a derivation of cosmology.

title: Acoustic speed from curvature equation of state
definition: Acoustic speed from curvature equation of state is the canonical CM19 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family. where the derivative must be taken along a specified thermodynamic or dynamical path.

title: Theta-based expansion ansatz
definition: Theta-based expansion ansatz is the canonical CM20 object in the Curvature-acoustic cosmology family. The constant \(K\) must carry the units needed for \(H^2\), and the equation requires independent derivation.

title: Closed spectral-loop representation
definition: Closed spectral-loop representation is the canonical TOP1 object in the Topology and spectral emergence family. This is the chosen configuration representation; emergence of the basis is a separate empirical claim.

title: WCT loop-energy functional
definition: WCT loop-energy functional is the canonical TOP2 object in the Topology and spectral emergence family.

title: Irreversible gradient flow
definition: Irreversible gradient flow is the canonical TOP3 object in the Topology and spectral emergence family. For a differentiable gradient flow, Well-posedness and treatment of self-avoidance require assumptions.
formula:
\partial_t\gamma = -\frac{\delta\mathcal E_{\rm loop}}{\delta\gamma}.

\frac{d\mathcal E_{\rm loop}}{dt} = - \left\| \frac{\delta\mathcal E_{\rm loop}}{\delta\gamma} \right\|^2 \le0.

title: Emergent-topology criterion
definition: Emergent-topology criterion is the canonical TOP4 object in the Topology and spectral emergence family. A proposed physical invariant \(I[\gamma]\) satisfies while

title: WCT dynamical codimension
formulas: \operatorname{codim}_{\rm WCT}(\gamma) := \text{minimum number of singular events required to reach the unknot}.
definition: WCT dynamical codimension is the canonical TOP5 object in the Topology and spectral emergence family. This is not manifold codimension.
formula: \operatorname{codim}_{\rm WCT}(\gamma) := \text{minimum number of singular events required to reach the unknot}.

title: Spectral topology bands
definition: Spectral topology bands is the canonical TOP6 object in the Topology and spectral emergence family. The claim that distinct knot types occupy disjoint asymptotic \(\epsilon\kappa\) bands requires broader numerical and experimental validation.

title: Topological mass proxy
definition: Topological mass proxy is the canonical TOP7 object in the Topology and spectral emergence family. Within a fixed topological and normalization class, The proportionality constant and absolute scale require calibration.

title: Holonomy non-invariance
definition: Holonomy non-invariance is the canonical TOP8 object in the Topology and spectral emergence family. For the proposed non-invariance statement is that smooth admissible deformations can change \(H\tau\) continuously. A general theorem for the declared flow class remains open.

title: Protein-particle structural correspondence
definition: Protein-particle structural correspondence is the canonical TOP9 object in the Topology and spectral emergence family. is a proposed analogy restricted to irreversible curvature flow with self-avoidance and spectral suppression. It is not an established physical equivalence.

title: Full Lyapunov candidate
definition: Full Lyapunov candidate is the canonical CORR1 object in the Canonical correction layer family. The curvature term alone is only one component.

title: Mean-amplitude spectral closure
definition: Mean-amplitude spectral closure is the canonical CORR2 object in the Canonical correction layer family. Under a weak-intermittency mean-amplitude approximation, This is an approximation, not an exact identity.

title: Spectral-weight notation
definition: Spectral-weight notation is the canonical CORR3 object in the Canonical correction layer family. Use for curvature cost and

title: Macro-micro control parameter
definition: Macro-micro control parameter is the canonical CORR4 object in the Canonical correction layer family.

title: Entropy-curvature coupling
definition: Entropy-curvature coupling is the canonical CORR5 object in the Canonical correction layer family. This remains the same open claim as E43 unless derived for a specified evolution.

title: Isoelectronic-flow alignment
definition: Isoelectronic-flow alignment is the canonical CORR6 object in the Canonical correction layer family. The imaginary-time isoelectronic flow is proposed as a reduced sector of M7 with ultraviolet smoothing and norm enforcement. A derivation of the reduction and its error bounds remains open.
